stories.title: Create monorepo directory structure
Add root Makefile with help, build, test, lint targets
Add root docker-compose.yml for local dev

Add .github/workflows/cd.yml — build and push on merge to main

Define qtown/common.proto — shared message types (Timestamp, Money, Pagination)
Define qtown/town_core.proto — WorldState, NPC, Building messages
Define qtown/market.proto — PriceEvent, TradeOrder, MarketSummary messages
Define qtown/fortress.proto — GuardEvent, ThreatLevel, PatrolRoute messages
Define qtown/academy.proto — LessonEvent, SkillGrant, CurriculumUpdate messages
Run buf generate and commit generated stubs for all languages
Scaffold infra/terraform/ with provider config and remote state
Add Terraform module for Postgres (Cloud SQL / RDS)
Add Terraform module for Redis (Cloud Memorystore / ElastiCache)
Add Terraform module for Kafka (Cloud MSK / Confluent)
Add Terraform module for container registry
Add Terraform module for Kubernetes cluster
Scaffold infra/k8s/base/ with namespace and RBAC manifests

Add Prometheus scrape config for all services
Add Grafana datasource provisioning (Prometheus, Loki, Tempo)
Add Grafana dashboard: service health overview
Add Grafana dashboard: Kafka consumer lag per topic
Add Prometheus alerting rules (service down, high error rate)
Scaffold ralph/ directory with v2_orchestrator.py skeleton
Implement v2_worklist.py — load, schedule, and persist worklist.json
Implement v2_model_router.py — route stories to Ollama models
Implement v2_cross_service.py — detect proto and multi-service stories
Add ralph/v2_config.py — centralised env-driven configuration
Add ralph/worklist.json — 194-story historical record
Add ralph/README.md — Ralph v2 architecture and operating guide
Scaffold services/town-core/ as FastAPI service with pyproject.toml
Add SQLAlchemy models: NPC, Building, WorldState, Transaction (v2 schema)
Add Alembic migration scaffolding and initial migration
Implement GET /api/world — returns full WorldState JSON
Implement POST /api/tick — advances simulation by one tick
Implement NPC lifecycle: spawn, age, die with Postgres-safe boolean columns
Implement NPC needs: hunger, energy, happiness decay and recovery
Implement NPC movement on 50×50 grid with home and work assignment
Implement building effects: hospital heals, school teaches, tavern restores energy
Implement economy: gold earning, taxation, treasury, inflation
Implement weather system: drought, rain, gold rush events
Add OpenTelemetry instrumentation to all town-core FastAPI routes
Add Prometheus /metrics endpoint to town-core
Add Dockerfile for town-core with multi-stage build
Publish WorldState events to Kafka topic qtown.world.ticked
Scaffold services/market-district/ as Go service with go.mod
Implement dynamic pricing engine: supply/demand curves, price history
Add REST API: GET /prices, POST /trade, GET /history/:resource
Implement gRPC MarketService with PriceStream and PlaceOrder RPCs
Consume qtown.world.ticked events from Kafka to update market state
Publish PriceEvent to Kafka topic qtown.market.price_updated
Add go test suite: pricing engine unit tests with race detector
Add OpenTelemetry tracing to market-district gRPC and HTTP handlers
Add Dockerfile for market-district with distroless base
Scaffold services/fortress/ as Rust service with Cargo.toml
Implement guard patrol scheduler: assign routes, detect threats
Implement threat level calculator: crime rate, external events, guard count
Add REST API: GET /threat-level, POST /incident, GET /patrols
Implement gRPC FortressService with GuardStream and ReportIncident RPCs
Add cargo test suite with clippy clean and zero-warning policy
Add OpenTelemetry tracing to fortress Actix-Web handlers
Publish GuardEvent to Kafka topic qtown.fortress.guard_event
Add Dockerfile for fortress with Rust multi-stage build
Wire town-core NPC crime rate to fortress threat calculator via Kafka
Wire market price shocks to town-core NPC buying behaviour via gRPC
Add integration test: full tick → market update → fortress response cycle
Add K8s Deployment and Service manifests for town-core
Add K8s Deployment and Service manifests for market-district
Add K8s Deployment and Service manifests for fortress
Add HorizontalPodAutoscaler for town-core (CPU and custom tick-rate metric)
Add Grafana dashboard: Phase 1 service health and tick latency
Run buf lint and buf generate after all Phase 1 proto updates
Scaffold services/academy/ as Python FastAPI service with pyproject.toml
Implement curriculum engine: skill trees, lesson plans, prerequisites
Implement NPC enrollment: assign NPCs to lessons based on role and skill gap
Implement lesson completion and SkillGrant event emission
Add REST API: GET /curriculum, POST /enroll, GET /skills/:npc_id
Implement gRPC AcademyService with SkillStream and EnrollNPC RPCs
Publish LessonEvent to Kafka topic qtown.academy.lesson_completed
Consume SkillGrant events in town-core to boost NPC productivity
Add pytest suite for academy curriculum and enrollment logic
Add Dockerfile for academy service
Scaffold services/tavern/ as TypeScript Fastify service with package.json
Implement NPC dialogue engine: template-based conversations with personality
Add REST API: GET /dialogue/:npc_id, POST /rumour, GET /menu
Implement rumour propagation: NPCs spread news across the tavern
Implement Kafka consumer: ingest WorldState events and generate rumours
Add WebSocket endpoint for real-time NPC dialogue stream
Add Jest test suite for dialogue engine and rumour propagation
Add Dockerfile for tavern service with Node.js Alpine base
Scaffold services/cartographer/ as TypeScript service with package.json
Implement map tile generator: 50×50 grid serialisation with building overlays
Add REST API: GET /map, GET /tile/:x/:y, GET /buildings-geojson
Implement path-finding: A* shortest path between two tiles
Add WebSocket endpoint: subscribe to NPC position updates
Consume town-core tick events to refresh NPC positions on map
Add Jest test suite for tile generator and path-finding
Add Dockerfile for cartographer service
Add K8s manifests for academy, tavern, cartographer
Add proto extension: qtown/tavern.proto — DialogueEvent, RumourMessage
Run buf generate after Phase 2 proto additions
Add Grafana dashboard: Phase 2 NPC activity and dialogue throughput
Scaffold services/library/ as Python service with FastAPI and pgvector
Implement document ingestion pipeline: chunk, embed, upsert to pgvector
Implement semantic search: query embedding → cosine similarity → top-k results
Add REST API: POST /ingest, GET /search?q=, GET /document/:id
Implement Kafka consumer: auto-ingest NPC dialogue and world events
Add connector: sync academy lesson content to library knowledge base
Add nomic-embed-text integration for embedding generation via Ollama
Add pytest suite for ingestion pipeline and semantic search
Add Dockerfile for library service
Scaffold services/asset-pipeline/ as Python service with Celery
Implement sprite generation worker: call Ollama vision model for NPC sprites
Implement building sprite generator with 16 building type templates
Add Celery task queue backed by Redis for async sprite jobs
Add REST API: POST /generate/npc, POST /generate/building, GET /status/:job_id
Implement S3-compatible object storage upload for generated assets
Add Kafka consumer: auto-generate sprites for new NPC roles and building types
Add workflow definitions for sprite generation pipelines (Temporal/Celery)
Add Dockerfile for asset-pipeline with GPU support annotation
Scaffold dashboard/ as Nuxt 3 app with Tailwind, Pinia, and nuxt.config.ts
Add dashboard layout: sidebar nav, top bar, main content area
Add map page: render 50×50 grid with PixiJS, consume cartographer WebSocket
Add NPC inspector panel: click NPC on map to view stats, history, relationships
Add market page: live price charts (Chart.js) from market-district WebSocket feed
Add tavern page: real-time NPC dialogue feed via WebSocket
Add knowledge search page: query library service and display results
Add admin controls: pause/resume tick, adjust simulation speed, trigger events
Add Pinia store: world-state, market-prices, active-npcs
Add nuxt server routes: BFF proxy for all backend service calls
Add Dockerfile for dashboard with Nuxt SSR build
Add K8s manifests for library, asset-pipeline, dashboard
Add Grafana dashboard: asset pipeline job queue depth and latency
Add Grafana dashboard: library search latency and embedding throughput
Add Ingress manifest for dashboard with TLS termination
Add HPA for asset-pipeline based on Celery queue depth custom metric
Add Helm chart values for Phase 3 services
Add end-to-end smoke test: dashboard loads, map renders, NPC inspector shows data
Add distributed trace propagation across all Kafka message boundaries
Add custom OTel span attributes: NPC ID, tick number, service version
Add OTel SDK to tavern (TypeScript) and cartographer (TypeScript)
Add OTel SDK to academy (Python) and library (Python)
Add OTel SDK to asset-pipeline (Python) with job trace context
Configure Tempo trace backend and Grafana Tempo datasource
Add Grafana dashboard: distributed trace heatmap and service dependency graph
Add JWT authentication middleware to all HTTP-facing services
Add RBAC: admin role for tick control and world reset; viewer role for reads
Add rate limiting to all public POST endpoints (token bucket via Redis)
Add input validation with Pydantic v2 strict mode on all Python services
Add Dependabot config for all package ecosystems (pip, go, npm, cargo)
Add container image scanning with Trivy in CI pipeline
Add mTLS via Linkerd for all gRPC connections between services
Add Rust criterion benchmark suite for fortress guard scheduler
Add Go benchmark suite for market-district pricing engine
Add k6 load test: 1000 concurrent tick requests to town-core
Add k6 load test: 500 concurrent price queries to market-district
Add library embedding throughput benchmark: target 100 embeds/sec
Add Grafana dashboard: p50/p95/p99 latency per service from k6 results
Add chaos engineering script: kill random service pod and verify recovery
Add PostgreSQL connection pooling via PgBouncer sidecar
Add Redis Sentinel config for high-availability Redis
Add Kafka topic retention and compaction policy config
Add Terraform workspace for production environment
Add GitHub Actions CD workflow: deploy to staging on merge to main
Add SLI/SLO definitions: 99.5% uptime, p95 tick latency < 200ms
Add runbooks: service-down recovery, Kafka consumer lag, OOM kill
Write root README.md: architecture diagram, quickstart, service table
Write docs/adr/001-go-for-market-district.md
Write docs/adr/002-rust-for-fortress.md
Write docs/adr/003-pgvector-over-dedicated-vectordb.md
Write docs/adr/004-kafka-for-event-bus.md
Write docs/adr/005-grpc-internal-rest-external.md
Write per-service README.md for all 9 services and dashboard
Write docs/architecture.md: C4 diagrams, data flows, technology choices
Add scripts/seed.sh: populate world with 50 NPCs, 20 buildings, starter economy
Add scripts/setup.sh: one-command dev environment setup
Add demo walkthrough script: automated tick sequence with narration output
Add dashboard loading screen and animated NPC sprite intro sequence
Add dashboard dark mode toggle with Tailwind dark: class support
Add dashboard mobile responsive layout for map and market pages
Run full test suite across all services and fix any regressions
Run buf lint and ensure proto/ has no breaking changes
Run cargo clippy --all with zero warnings on fortress
Run go vet ./... with zero errors on market-district
Run ruff check on all Python services with zero violations
Tag v2.0.0 release: update CHANGELOG.md, create GitHub release with artifact links
Architect town-core gRPC server: define proto contracts and Tonic-style server in Python
stories.description: Cartographer's grpc-clients instantiate a town-core client but town-core only exposes HTTP. Define proto contract for ListNPCs, GetWorld, GetNPC, GetBuildings; generate Python stubs into services/town-core/engine/__generated__/; add grpc.aio server alongside FastAPI in services/town-core/engine/main.py listening on a new port (e.g. 50050).
acceptance_criteria: proto/town_core.proto exists with all RPCs cartographer's resolvers.ts calls
services/town-core/engine/grpc_server.py implements TownCoreServicer
services/town-core/engine/main.py starts grpc server alongside uvicorn
cartographer integration test calls town-core.ListNPCs and gets non-empty data

stories.title: Architect Fortress proto codegen via tonic-build and mount Tonic gRPC router
stories.description: services/fortress/src/main.rs:127 has 'proto codegen pending' placeholder. Wire build.rs with tonic-build pointing at proto/fortress.proto. Generate Rust types into src/__generated__/. Mount the Tonic router in main.rs alongside the Kafka consumer. Expose ValidateEvent RPC.
acceptance_criteria: services/fortress/build.rs invokes tonic-build with proto/fortress.proto
services/fortress/src/grpc_server.rs implements ValidateEvent
services/fortress/src/main.rs starts tonic Server on port 50052
cargo test passes; integration test from town-core calls fortress.ValidateEvent and gets accept/reject response

stories.title: Refactor Fortress to confine all unsafe blocks to a single wasm wrapper module
stories.description: README claims zero unsafe in validation/rules. grep finds 27 unsafe blocks across services/fortress/src/. Audit each; move any required unsafe (likely from wasmtime FFI) into src/wasm_sandbox/unsafe_ffi.rs as the only file allowed to contain unsafe. Add a CI check (similar to existing zero-unsafe check in .github/workflows/ci.yml) that confirms unsafe count outside that file is zero.
acceptance_criteria: All unsafe blocks live in services/fortress/src/wasm_sandbox/unsafe_ffi.rs
Existing CI grep check is updated to cover all of src/, allowing only that one file
cargo clippy -- -D warnings passes
cargo test passes

stories.title: Architect Library gRPC server for RAG queries from Academy
stories.description: Academy needs Library context for RAG-grounded NPC content. Define proto/library.proto with QueryRAG (text, k, filter) → ranked passages. Implement the server in services/library/library/grpc_server.py using ES vector search; mount in services/library/library/main.py alongside HTTP.
acceptance_criteria: proto/library.proto declares QueryRAG RPC
services/library/library/grpc_server.py implements LibraryServicer
services/library/library/main.py starts gRPC alongside FastAPI
Academy integration test calls library.QueryRAG and gets ranked passages

stories.title: Register market-district gRPC service in cmd/server/main.go
stories.description: services/market-district/cmd/server/main.go:34 has TODO: register gRPC service implementations. Wire grpc_marketdistrict.RegisterMarketDistrictServer(grpcServer, &grpc.MarketServer{...}) so PlaceOrder is actually reachable.
acceptance_criteria: cmd/server/main.go registers the service against the grpc.Server before Serve()
go test ./... passes
An external client (or the existing integration_test.go) successfully calls PlaceOrder over the network

stories.title: Emit qtown.economy.trade.settled from market-district after each successful match
stories.description: services/market-district/internal/grpc/server.go:79 has TODO: emit economy.trade.settled. Add a sarama or kgo producer in internal/kafka/producer.go; emit one event per matched trade with fields trade_id, buyer_npc_id, seller_npc_id, item, qty, price, gold_delta. Town-core's existing handler at services/town-core/engine/kafka_consumer.py:115-137 consumes this topic.
acceptance_criteria: internal/kafka/producer.go publishes a struct serialized to JSON or proto on topic qtown.economy.trade.settled
Producer is invoked from internal/grpc/server.go in the same place the TODO removed
After PlaceOrder + match, town-core consumer logs a trade and updates the buyer/seller wallet

stories.title: Add Kafka producer in academy emitting qtown.ai.content.generated after GenerateDialogue
stories.description: Tavern's services/tavern/src/kafka-consumer.ts:220-224 already subscribes to ai.content.generated but academy never produces it. Add aiokafka producer in services/academy/academy/kafka_producer.py; emit on every successful GenerateDialogue with fields conversation_id, npc_a, npc_b, lines, model, generated_at.
acceptance_criteria: services/academy/academy/kafka_producer.py created with aiokafka producer
academy/grpc_server.py calls producer after Ollama generates dialogue
Tavern integration test consumes the event and broadcasts to a connected WS client

stories.title: Add town-core consumer handler for qtown.validation.result
stories.description: Fortress emits validation results to qtown.validation.result. No service consumes them. Register a handler in services/town-core/engine/kafka_consumer.py that reads (event_id, accepted: bool, reason) and either commits or rolls back the pending state mutation referenced by event_id.
acceptance_criteria: services/town-core/engine/kafka_consumer.py registers a handler for qtown.validation.result
town-core has a pending-mutations table or in-memory map keyed by event_id
Integration test: emit validation.request, fortress accepts, town-core commits the change; emit again, fortress rejects, town-core rolls back

stories.title: Subscribe asset-pipeline to NPC and building creation events to trigger sprite generation
stories.description: asset-pipeline has a Kafka consumer task but it doesn't subscribe to creation events. Wire to qtown.entity.npc_created and qtown.entity.building_created. On each event, enqueue a ComfyUI workflow with the entity attributes; on completion, emit qtown.entity.asset_ready with the sprite URL.
acceptance_criteria: Consumer subscribes to both topics
Each event triggers comfyui_client.submit_workflow with templated params
On completion, asset-pipeline emits qtown.entity.asset_ready with public URL
Dashboard's NPC list shows generated sprites for newly created NPCs

stories.title: Add NPC order origination in town-core that calls market-district PlaceOrder via gRPC
stories.description: No service initiates trades today. NPCs at the market should call market-district.PlaceOrder when their goal selector chooses 'buy food' or 'sell goods'. Add a grpc client in services/town-core/engine/grpc_clients/market_client.py; have services/town-core/engine/simulation/npcs.py call it from the relevant action handler.
acceptance_criteria: Market gRPC client exists in town-core
NPC action handler for 'buy_at_market' / 'sell_at_market' invokes PlaceOrder
After a tick, market-district logs received orders; matches happen; trade.settled events flow

stories.title: Add NPC dialogue trigger endpoint in town-core that calls academy GenerateDialogue
stories.description: Academy's GenerateDialogue is reachable but nothing calls it. When two NPCs end up adjacent during a tick (already detected by the gossip system), call academy.GenerateDialogue and dispatch the result via the existing dialogues.py router. The router currently exists but uses static text.
acceptance_criteria: services/town-core/engine/grpc_clients/academy_client.py created
services/town-core/engine/simulation/npcs.py invokes academy on adjacent-NPC events
services/town-core/engine/routers/dialogues.py returns AI-generated lines
Dashboard chat bubble shows the generated dialogue end-to-end

stories.title: Add gRPC client in academy to query Library for RAG context before Ollama generation
stories.description: Academy generates dialogue without retrieving the relevant town history. Add a Library gRPC client in services/academy/academy/library_client.py. In GenerateDialogue, before calling Ollama, query Library.QueryRAG for the top-k recent events involving the two NPCs and inject the passages into the prompt.
acceptance_criteria: services/academy/academy/library_client.py created
GenerateDialogue prompt includes RAG context
Integration test: indexed event 'NPC A insulted NPC B yesterday' shows up in subsequent dialogue context

stories.title: Define and compile academy LangGraph workflow for NPC decision agents
stories.description: langgraph is in pyproject.toml but no StatefulGraph or compiled graph exists. Build services/academy/academy/agents/npc_decision_graph.py with nodes: gather_context, query_rag, choose_action, generate_dialogue. Compile and expose via a gRPC RPC.
acceptance_criteria: services/academy/academy/agents/npc_decision_graph.py defines a StateGraph and compiles it
A new gRPC RPC RunNPCAgent invokes the compiled graph
Tests cover at least three branches of the graph

stories.title: Implement library indexing pipeline: Kafka consumer writes events into ES per template
stories.description: Library has ES connect and templates but no actual indexing pipeline. Wire the existing Kafka consumer task to read from qtown.* topics and index each event into the matching ES index using the templates from index_templates.py.
acceptance_criteria: services/library/library/indexer.py implements per-topic write logic
After producing N events on Kafka, GET /_cat/indices on ES shows them populated
Tests use testcontainers ES + Kafka

stories.title: Implement library search and aggregation query endpoints
stories.description: FastAPI app exists but no search endpoints implemented. Add /search/full-text, /search/timeline, /aggregate/npc-history endpoints. These power the Cartographer GraphQL Library type.
acceptance_criteria: Three endpoints implemented with ES query DSL
Cartographer's library resolver calls them and returns data to GraphQL clients
Dashboard /newspaper page shows ES-aggregated event timeline

stories.title: Wire asset-pipeline output to S3 (or local MinIO in dev) with public URLs
stories.description: ComfyUI generates sprites but no CDN delivery. Add services/asset-pipeline/asset_pipeline/storage.py wrapping boto3 (S3) with a MinIO fallback for docker-compose dev. Upload generated sprites under a versioned path; return public URL.
acceptance_criteria: storage.py supports both S3 and MinIO
docker-compose.yml adds minio service for dev
Generated sprite URL is reachable from the dashboard's in tests

stories.title: Investigate cartographer grpc-clients tryLoadPackage soft-fail and replace with strict load
stories.description: services/cartographer/src/grpc-clients.ts:24-35 returns null on missing proto. That hides bugs. Replace with strict load that throws a diagnostic error naming which proto file failed and where it was looked for. If the design intent really is graceful degradation, gate it behind GRACEFUL_GRPC=1 env.
acceptance_criteria: Default behavior is strict load with clear error
Optional env-gated graceful mode preserved
Existing tests still pass; a new test asserts the diagnostic error message

stories.title: Override dashboard port from 3000 to 3100 in docker-compose to free Kevin's reserved local-dev range
stories.description: Ports 3000-3010 are reserved for Kevin's other long-running services. docker-compose.yml exposes dashboard on 3000. Override to 3100 host-side; container can stay on 3000 internally.
acceptance_criteria: docker-compose.yml maps 3100:3000 for dashboard
Makefile target make docker-up still brings up dashboard reachable at http://localhost:3100
README port map reflects new host port

stories.title: Design v2 production deployment: pick host (Railway / Fly / k8s) and ship minimum subset to qtown.ai
stories.description: Currently no v2 service is deployed. Decide on the minimum-viable production footprint (probably cartographer + dashboard + one backing service + Postgres + Redis), pick a host, and ship. Existing infra/helm and infra/terraform are k8s-flavored but k8s is overkill for a portfolio piece. Likely choice: Fly.io for the Go/TS services, with managed Postgres/Redis. Output: a docs/v2-deploy.md plan and the actual config commit.
acceptance_criteria: docs/v2-deploy.md documents the chosen platform and tradeoffs
A working preview deployment exists at a temporary URL
qtown.ai is flipped from the Vercel coming-soon to the v2 dashboard once auth/health checks are clean

stories.title: Architect world snapshot system in town-core for periodic state export and replay
stories.description: v1 had snapshot PNGs of the simulation every N ticks. v2 has none. Implement a snapshotter that runs alongside the tick loop, dumps key state (npcs, buildings, world tiles, time, weather) to JSON in services/town-core/snapshots/ and renders a 50x50 PNG of the grid using PIL. Surfaces via /api/snapshots/latest.
acceptance_criteria: services/town-core/engine/snapshotter.py implements both JSON + PNG snapshot
Background task fires every N ticks (configurable via env)
GET /api/snapshots/latest returns metadata + signed PNG URL

stories.title: Migrate v1 sprite assets into asset-pipeline storage so dashboard can render them
stories.description: v1's assets/ contains hand-crafted building and NPC sprites still useful for v2 fallbacks. Migrate them into the asset-pipeline storage layer, register their URLs, and have town-core link NPCs/buildings to a sprite_url so the dashboard can render even before generated sprites are ready.
acceptance_criteria: Migration script uploads everything in assets/{npcs,buildings} to the storage layer
Town-core models hold sprite_url with a v1-asset default
Dashboard NPC table shows sprites for every NPC

stories.title: Investigate market-trade flow end-to-end and write integration test covering all 7 hops
stories.description: Write tests/integration/test_market_trade_flow.py using testcontainers. Boot Kafka + Postgres + Redis + town-core + market-district + tavern. Place an order via gRPC, assert match happens, assert trade.settled event lands on Kafka, assert town-core wallet update, assert tavern WS broadcast, assert dashboard GraphQL query returns the trade.
acceptance_criteria: Integration test passes locally and in CI
Failure at any hop produces a clear assertion message naming the hop
Test runs in <60s wall clock

stories.title: Investigate AI dialogue flow end-to-end and write integration test
stories.description: Write tests/integration/test_dialogue_flow.py. Trigger an NPC adjacency event in town-core, assert academy.GenerateDialogue is called, assert library RAG was consulted, assert ai.content.generated landed on Kafka, assert tavern broadcasted, assert dashboard rendered the chat bubble.
acceptance_criteria: Test passes with stubbed Ollama (use a deterministic small model or mock at the HTTP layer)
Each hop has a named assertion
Test runs in <90s wall clock

stories.title: Investigate validation flow end-to-end and confirm Fortress accept/reject is acted on
stories.description: Write tests/integration/test_validation_flow.py. Town-core proposes a state mutation, validation.request goes to fortress, fortress validates via WASM, validation.result returns, town-core either commits or rolls back.
acceptance_criteria: Both accept and reject paths are tested
On reject, town-core state is unchanged
On accept, town-core state reflects the mutation

stories.title: Update README v2 architecture diagram to mark live vs stubbed gRPC and Kafka topics
stories.description: README claims a fully wired polyglot system. Update the architecture section to mark each gRPC link and Kafka topic with status (LIVE / STUB / PENDING) and progressively flip them to LIVE as Phase 6 stories close.
acceptance_criteria: README arch diagram annotated
A status table maps each topic + RPC to a status
After Phase 6 closes, all entries are LIVE

stories.title: Update landing page stats to reflect Phase-6 truth on qtown.ai
stories.description: landing/index.html currently says '194 stories shipped'. Once Phase 6 ships, replace with the cumulative count and a live-from-v2 verb tense. Coordinate with P6-019 (production deploy).
acceptance_criteria: Hero stats reflect v2 reality (e.g. '220 stories', topics live, services healthy)
Live data section now pulls from v2 cartographer once it's deployed
Coming-soon framing removed from hero copy

stories.title: Build ghz load-test harness for market-district PlaceOrder gRPC path
stories.description: The README claims '<5ms p99 at 10K orders/sec' and it has never been run under load. Create perf/market-district/ with a ghz (https://ghz.sh) script driving the gRPC PlaceOrder path under docker-compose. Include warmup, 3 measured runs, and recorded hardware/notes. perf/run.sh must reproduce deterministically on Kevin's machine.
acceptance_criteria: perf/market-district/run.sh exists and reproduces on a fresh checkout
Script does warmup + 3 runs at stepped load (1K/5K/10K orders/sec)
Raw ghz JSON output committed under perf/market-district/results/

stories.title: Write perf/REPORT.md with measured p50/p99/p999 and saturation analysis
stories.description: Author perf/REPORT.md: methodology, exact hardware, p50/p99/p999 at 1K/5K/10K orders/sec, where the engine saturates, and a flamegraph of the hot path. The measured number wins interviews; the claimed one loses them. Numbers must come from P6.5-001 output, not estimates.
acceptance_criteria: perf/REPORT.md committed with real numbers traceable to results/ JSON
Saturation point and flamegraph (or pprof svg) included
Every number cites the run that produced it

stories.title: Rewrite README + landing perf claims to the measured number
stories.description: Replace every unmeasured perf claim in README and dashboard landing with the measured value from perf/REPORT.md (whatever it is). The Market room proof panel (Plan 01 §4.1) reads the same constants from a single committed source so copy and panel can never diverge.
acceptance_criteria: No unmeasured perf claim remains anywhere in the repo (grep clean)
README perf line links to perf/REPORT.md
A single perf-constants source feeds README + Market proof panel

stories.title: Add k6 gateway load script for GraphQL townOverview fan-out
stories.description: Add a k6 script hitting the cartographer GraphQL townOverview fan-out at 100 rps — the user-facing latency number. Commit the k6 summary alongside perf/REPORT.md.
acceptance_criteria: perf/gateway/townOverview.k6.js exists
k6 summary JSON committed; p95 fan-out latency recorded in REPORT.md

stories.title: Standardize /healthz + /readyz across all 9 services
stories.description: Today there is no uniform health story. Every service must answer two questions the same way: /healthz (process up) and /readyz (dependencies reachable — Kafka producer connected, ES green, Ollama tags listed, Redis ping). Use the gRPC Health Checking Protocol for market-district + fortress; HTTP for the rest. readyz lists each dependency with its own status.
acceptance_criteria: make health prints a 9-row status table
Each service exposes both endpoints; readyz enumerates per-dependency status
market-district + fortress implement grpc.health.v1.Health

stories.title: Aggregate town-core GET /system/health fanning out to all services
stories.description: town-core GET /system/health fans out to all 9 service health endpoints and returns the matrix. This is the Clinic room's feed (Plan 01 §4.2): the examination table renders real statuses and a sick patient NPC appears when a service is degraded.
acceptance_criteria: GET /system/health returns a 9-service status matrix
Killing tavern turns its row red within 15s
Response shape documented for the Clinic ProofPanel

stories.title: CI job that proves world-snapshot restore actually works
stories.description: P6-020's snapshot system gets a CI job: snapshot a world, wipe a scratch world, restore, and diff. The Bank vault panel's 'restore-tested ✅' must be earned, not asserted. NEVER run against any real/durable data — scratch world only.
acceptance_criteria: CI job snapshots -> wipes scratch -> restores -> diffs clean
Job fails if restore diverges from snapshot
Runs only against an ephemeral scratch world (no durable data touched)

stories.title: Instrument town-core (Python) with OpenTelemetry spans
stories.description: Jaeger/Prometheus/Grafana are configured in infra/ but no service emits spans. Add the OTel SDK to town-core: tick loop, gRPC server, and Kafka produce. Inject W3C traceparent into Kafka message headers so the trace can continue downstream.
acceptance_criteria: Spans for tick loop + gRPC + Kafka produce visible in Jaeger
traceparent injected into Kafka headers on every produced message

stories.title: Instrument market-district (Go) continuing the inbound trace context
stories.description: Add OTel to market-district: a gRPC server interceptor and Kafka emit instrumentation that continues the trace context arriving from town-core. One trace ID must span town-core -> market.
acceptance_criteria: One trace ID spans town-core -> market-district in Jaeger
Kafka emit propagates the same traceparent forward

stories.title: Instrument tavern (TS) closing the polyglot market-trade trace
stories.description: Add OTel to tavern: Kafka consume -> WS broadcast, continuing the same trace. The result is one market-trade trace crossing 3 languages and gRPC + Kafka hops as a single Jaeger waterfall.
acceptance_criteria: Full market-trade trace: 3 languages, gRPC + Kafka hops, one waterfall
tavern consumer span is a child of the market emit span

stories.title: Read-only Jaeger query proxy + commit the trace screenshot
stories.description: Add a small read-only proxy for the Jaeger query API (Jaeger is never exposed raw) so the Tower observation deck (P7-016) can render the live waterfall. Commit the trace screenshot to docs/img/trace-market-flow.png for the README.
acceptance_criteria: Read-only proxy returns the latest trace tagged flow=market-trade
docs/img/trace-market-flow.png committed and referenced in README

stories.title: Wire kafka-exporter -> Prometheus -> Warehouse room feed
stories.description: Add kafka-exporter to the existing Prometheus config so per-topic throughput and consumer lag are scraped. This is the Warehouse room's feed: holo-tags show real lag numbers (Plan 01 §4.2).
acceptance_criteria: kafka-exporter scraped by Prometheus; per-topic lag query returns data
A read-only endpoint exposes topic throughput/lag for the Warehouse panel

stories.title: Add gitleaks secret-scanning CI gate
stories.description: Add a required gitleaks job to .github/workflows/ci.yml. This is the secrets postmortem's structural guard (commit 280d792 leaked keys). The gate must block merge on any detected secret.
acceptance_criteria: gitleaks job is a required check on every PR
CI fails on a seeded fake secret in a test PR
Baseline/allowlist documented if any historical match is intentionally ignored

stories.title: Add trivy container/image vulnerability scan CI gate
stories.description: Add a required trivy scan over built images to ci.yml, failing on HIGH/CRITICAL with a documented, time-boxed allowlist mechanism.
acceptance_criteria: trivy job required; fails on a seeded HIGH/CRITICAL vuln
Allowlist file documented with expiry dates

stories.title: Add buf breaking-change gate on proto/
stories.description: Add a required `buf breaking` check against the main branch baseline so proto contract changes can't silently break a consumer.
acceptance_criteria: buf breaking job required; fails on a seeded incompatible proto change
buf.yaml/buf.gen.yaml baseline configured

stories.title: Add per-language dependency-audit CI gates
stories.description: Add dependency vulnerability audits per language as required checks: pip-audit (Python), govulncheck (Go), cargo audit (Rust), npm audit --audit-level=high (TS).
acceptance_criteria: All four audit jobs run and are required
Each fails on a seeded known-vulnerable dependency

stories.title: Make the 3 e2e flow tests a required merge gate (Gate A wiring in CI)
stories.description: Add the `e2e` job to ci.yml: boot docker-compose.deps.yml + the services each flow needs, run test_market_trade_flow / test_ai_dialogue_flow / test_validation_flow, required for merge. Slow (~5-8 min) is acceptable; wrongness is not. Verify by intentionally breaking one hop and watching CI fail.
acceptance_criteria: e2e job required on every PR; all 3 flows green on main
Intentionally breaking one hop in a PR turns the check red
README architecture diagram drops the live-vs-stubbed marker once green

stories.title: Author 4 ComfyUI workflow JSONs (classes A-D) with Flux + IP-Adapter + ControlNet
stories.description: workflows/ has only INSTALL.md. Author 4 ComfyUI workflow JSONs (one per asset class A-D) per the INSTALL.md model layout: Flux.1-dev fp8, IP-Adapter (identity), ControlNet-OpenPose (activity poses), with parameterized prompt/size/seed inputs so run_batch.py can drive them.
acceptance_criteria: 4 workflow JSONs validate via the ComfyUI /prompt API dry-run
Prompt/size/seed are externally parameterized inputs
IP-Adapter present in B/D workflows; ControlNet-OpenPose present in D

stories.title: Add interior_cast matrix + class-E extras to taxonomy.yaml (additive only)
stories.description: Add the curated per-room (role x activity) cast matrix as a new ADDITIVE interior_cast field on each room in taxonomy.yaml (taxonomy stays locked — this adds, never changes), plus the class-E extras list (ground tiles, props, landing hero, OG, favicon). Curation rule: 1-2 roles per activity. Dedupe by (role, activity).
acceptance_criteria: run_batch.py --plan prints the full manifest with counts matching Plan 02 §1.6 ±10%
taxonomy.yaml `locked: true` preserved; only additive fields added
Class-D unique sprite count resolves to ~95-110 after dedupe

stories.title: Finish run_batch.py: manifest expansion, submit/poll/download, retry, filters
stories.description: Complete run_batch.py: expand the manifest from taxonomy, submit jobs to ComfyUI, poll, download, retry-on-failure, --only-new, --only-class, and deterministic seeds logged per asset. Reads taxonomy at run time (no second source of truth).
acceptance_criteria: run_batch.py --mode test --limit 10 produces 10 correctly named files
--only-new and --only-class filters work
Every generation logs prompt+seed+workflow+model-hash to output/genlog.jsonl

stories.title: Post-process: rembg transparency, WebP convert, per-class contact sheets
stories.description: Add postprocess.py: rembg for class B/D transparency, WebP conversion, and a contact-sheet generator (one grid PNG per class) for Kevin's QA review.
acceptance_criteria: postprocess.py runs clean on the test batch
Contact sheets render one grid per class
B/D outputs have clean transparent edges

stories.title: Emit assets/manifest.json consumed by the dashboard InteriorRenderer
stories.description: Emit assets/manifest.json mapping building/room/sprite -> relative path, served via asset-pipeline storage (P6-016) and consumed by the dashboard (Plan 01 §5). The dashboard must render any room with zero hardcoded asset paths.
acceptance_criteria: manifest.json covers 100% of taxonomy rooms/roles
Dashboard InteriorRenderer loads a room purely from the manifest
Manifest schema documented for the dashboard composable

stories.title: LoRA A/B decision: evaluate solarpunk LoRA vs none, record in DECISIONS.md
stories.description: Run A/B test-gens with vs without a solarpunk LoRA from config.yaml's candidate slots; pick or drop. Record the decision + seeds in asset-gen/DECISIONS.md.
acceptance_criteria: A/B contact sheet generated for both arms
Decision + seeds + reasoning recorded in asset-gen/DECISIONS.md

stories.title: Architect room/activity projection in town-core (rooms.yaml + simulation/rooms.py)
stories.description: The sim does not track rooms. Add a projection layer (NOT a new simulation system): rooms.yaml generated from taxonomy.yaml (single source of truth, synced by script), simulation/rooms.py with Room{building_id,room_id,activities,capacity}, NPC gains current_building/current_room/current_activity/activity_since, and a deterministic action->(building,room,activity) mapping table living in rooms.yaml.
acceptance_criteria: Unit test: every taxonomy room is reachable
Unit test: every sim action maps to a valid (building, room, activity)
rooms.yaml is generated from taxonomy.yaml, never hand-duplicated

stories.title: Add rooms.proto + buf codegen (Python + TS)
stories.description: Add proto/qtown/town/v1/rooms.proto with RoomState, Occupant, GetRoomStateRequest/Response. Run buf codegen for Python (town-core) + TS (cartographer).
acceptance_criteria: buf generate is clean
Generated stubs imported by town-core + cartographer

stories.title: Emit qtown.town.room.activity per tick; register topic in kafka-init
stories.description: town-core emits qtown.town.room.activity to Kafka once per tick (30s), one message per OCCUPIED room: {building_id,room_id,occupants:[{npc_id,role,activity,since}],tick}. Register the topic in infra/kafka-init.sh.
acceptance_criteria: Topic shows >=1 msg/tick with valid schema in an integration test
Topic registered in kafka-init.sh
Only occupied rooms emit (no empty-room spam)

stories.title: Implement GetRoomState RPC on the town-core gRPC server
stories.description: Implement the GetRoomState RPC on town-core's gRPC server (P6-001) returning live occupants for a building, for the dashboard's initial page load.
acceptance_criteria: grpcurl GetRoomState returns occupants matching sim state
Empty building returns an empty-but-valid response, not an error

stories.title: Add cartographer roomState(buildingId) GraphQL query
stories.description: Add a cartographer roomState(buildingId) query resolving to town-core's GetRoomState gRPC. Fail loud if town-core is down (the P6-017 strict-load pattern) — no soft-fail.
acceptance_criteria: GraphQL roomState query returns live data
Resolver errors loudly when town-core is unreachable (no silent empty)

stories.title: Add tavern room:<building> WS channels from the new topic
stories.description: tavern consumes qtown.town.room.activity and broadcasts to a new WS channel room:<building_id> (the pattern already exists in src/kafka-consumer.ts).
acceptance_criteria: A WS test client subscribed to room:tavern receives deltas within 1 tick
Channel name pattern matches the dashboard composable's expectation

stories.title: Drill-in route + ZoomTransition + overhead click-targets
stories.description: Add the full-page route /town/<building_id> (deep-linkable, browser-history aware). Overhead camera tweens to the building footprint (~400ms) then crossfades to the interior; Esc/back-arrow/browser-back zooms out. No modal — interiors are a place. Add building click-targets + a hover affordance to the existing PixiRenderer.
acceptance_criteria: Click tavern -> animated zoom -> /town/tavern interior; back returns
Deep-link to /town/tavern loads the interior directly
Esc and browser-back both zoom out

stories.title: InteriorRenderer: 2.5D parallax scene + NPC sprite layer + tween system
stories.description: New PixiJS InteriorRenderer.client.vue: side-view dollhouse with parallax layers (background plate -> mid props -> NPC layer -> foreground accents at differing scroll factors). Liveness via cheap tweens (idle bob ±2px, walk slide, flip) + speech bubbles — not sprite-sheet animation. Must hit the Plan 01 §3.7 perf budget.
acceptance_criteria: Renders a room from manifest assets at 60fps with 15 sprites (M-series)
<=2.5MB transferred per room first-visit; LRU cache of 3 visited rooms
Overhead view unloads while inside

stories.title: useRoomState composable: GraphQL snapshot + WS delta merge + dormant mode
stories.description: composables/useRoomState.ts: initial snapshot via GraphQL roomState, live updates via WS room:<id>. When a feed is absent, return dormant state (never fabricate occupants).
acceptance_criteria: Occupants update without a page refresh
Dormant mode returns when the feed is absent (no fabricated data)

stories.title: ProofPanel component + per-room panel configs (no-fake-data enforced)
stories.description: components/ProofPanel.vue, data-driven by dashboard/proof-panels/<building>.<room>.ts ({title,metrics:[{label,source,format,staleAfterSec}],docsLink}). Hard rules: no fabricated values ever (errored source -> '—' + reason on hover); staleness visible (dim + age past staleAfterSec); dormant mode names the missing gate; every panel links to its ops page + room doc.
acceptance_criteria: Panel renders real metrics for all 5 flagship rooms
An errored metric source renders '—' with reason, never a fabricated value
Stale values dim and show their age

stories.title: RoomDocs drawer + content loader
stories.description: components/RoomDocs.vue: the ⓘ drawer that loads docs/rooms/<building>.<room>.md (content authored in Plan 04 §6) for the room currently in view.
acceptance_criteria: ⓘ opens the room's doc drawer with the right content
Drawer closes on Esc and does not block the scene

A room may not fake its feed — if the backing flow isn't green yet, ship dormant (labeled, NPCs idle, panel says 'awaiting Phase 6 wiring').

stories.title: Write ADR-10 Agent-written code: CI as the trust boundary
stories.description: Draft ADR-10 Agent-written code: CI as the trust boundary using the template Context -> Decision -> Consequences -> Receipts. Receipt that makes it credible: the e2e gates (Plan 03 §1); the audit. <=1 page, zero aspirational statements; every Consequences claim links to a receipt. Kevin voice-passes before merge.
acceptance_criteria: ADR is <=1 page
Every Consequences claim links to a real receipt (code/test/report)
No aspirational/unproven statements

stories.title: Write docs/agent-ops.md — the differentiator document
stories.description: Nobody else interviews with this doc. Cover: Ralph architecture (orchestrator, worklist, conflict detection) · the model-routing policy as production config · the HUMAN.md steering protocol + intervention log · cost-per-story methodology · THE AUDIT STORY (194/194 'complete' while 0/3 flows worked — 'true per-story, misleading at the system level' — and the structural fix: e2e gates) · how Opus + Ralph divide work in this very plan pack (the doc describes the process that produced it).
acceptance_criteria: agent-ops.md covers all six sections with real artifacts linked
The audit story is told honestly with the structural fix
The Opus-plans / Ralph-executes / Opus-cleanup cadence is documented

stories.title: Write SECURITY.md + STRIDE-lite threat model
stories.description: SECURITY.md: trust-boundary diagram (public -> cartographer -> mesh; Kafka internal; Ollama/ComfyUI local-only), what's enforced today (Linkerd mTLS/authz), and what's honestly NOT done (no auth on internal RPCs, no gateway rate limit — with the P8-003 plan attached). Plus a 1-page STRIDE-lite threat model mapping mitigations to the CI gates and OWASP-LLM items. Honest partial > silent absence.
acceptance_criteria: SECURITY.md states enforced vs not-done honestly with linked plans
Threat model maps each mitigation to a CI gate or OWASP-LLM item

acceptance_criteria: Postmortem covers leak, blast radius, rotation timeline, and CI guards
Rotation facts confirmed by Kevin before merge

stories.title: Build MCP server exposing read-only town tools
stories.description: services/mcp-gateway (or a town-core module): an MCP server exposing read-only tools — query_town_state, get_npc, get_room_activity, search_library, get_trace_summary. Python or TS SDK, stdio + SSE transports. The 2026 table-stakes integration story. Tower's holo-array gains an 'agent uplink' visual + MCP docs hook.
acceptance_criteria: Claude Code connects and answers 'who is in the tavern right now' from live state
Both stdio and SSE transports work
All exposed tools are read-only (no mutation surface)

stories.title: LLM evals in CI: golden dialogue set + local judge + threshold gate
stories.description: evals/: ~30-case golden dialogue set, rubric scoring via a local judge model (deepseek-r1:14b), thresholds as a CI gate behind a recorded-fixture mode for determinism. 'How do you test LLM output' is a guaranteed interview probe. Academy laboratory room renders the latest eval run.
acceptance_criteria: A deliberately degraded prompt fails the CI eval gate
Eval gate is deterministic in CI via recorded fixtures
Rubric + thresholds documented

stories.title: Gateway hardening: API-key auth, rate limit, query-depth/complexity limits
stories.description: cartographer: API-key auth for mutating ops, a Redis sliding-window rate limit, and GraphQL query-depth + complexity limits. 'Your gateway is the front door.'
acceptance_criteria: A k6 abuse script gets 429s
A depth-bomb query is rejected by the depth/complexity limiter
Mutating ops require a valid API key

stories.title: Structured outputs: academy emits schema-validated dialogue JSON
stories.description: academy emits schema-validated JSON (Ollama format=json + pydantic validation + retry-on-invalid). NPC dialogue gets {speaker,text,mood,refs[]}; mood drives the NPC sprite pose in the classroom projector.
acceptance_criteria: Invalid-schema rate <1% over the eval set
mood field drives the rendered NPC pose
Invalid generations are retried, never emitted raw

stories.title: Guardrails: moderation as a fortress WASM rule + prompt-injection canaries
stories.description: Add a moderation pass on qtown.ai.content.generated AS a fortress WASM rule (content policy = just another validation), plus prompt-injection canary tests on the RAG path. Rejected content gets adjudicated in the Citadel — on theme. OWASP-LLM coverage.
acceptance_criteria: Disallowed generated content is rejected by the WASM moderation rule
Prompt-injection canary cases are caught on the RAG path
Rejected content surfaces in the Courthouse docket

stories.title: Hybrid retrieval: ES dense-vector kNN + BM25 + reranker, A/B on the eval set
stories.description: library: ES dense-vector kNN (nomic embeddings) + BM25 + the existing reranker; A/B on the eval set. Library console gains a retrieval-mode toggle.
acceptance_criteria: Hybrid retrieval beats BM25-only on the eval set (recorded A/B)
Retrieval-mode toggle works in the Library console

stories.title: Streaming token UX: Ollama stream -> academy -> WS -> classroom projector

acceptance_criteria: Classroom projector renders generation token-by-token over WS
Stream cleanly terminates and finalizes the message
